tasks.name: preparePublicKeyForDeployment + pkToNttCompact + G5 KAT (A-007 structural oracle)
G6 happy-path validateUserOp + gas-cap + AC-FLOW-1 (100 KAT + 5 fresh-keypair)
G6 failure paths + shared SignatureMalformed walker extraction (AC-4/AC-5a/AC-5b/AC-6)
5-scheme bench + SCHEME_DEPLOYERS registry + pairwise delta report + AC-10 naming grep (AC-7/8/9/10/13)

patternsEstablished: A-007 structural-reconciliation oracle for G5 pk-transform: fixture encodes as uint256[32] (1024 B, Python ref sig_sol.py:48), preparePublicKeyForDeployment emits uint256[] (1088 B, on-chain setKey input). Test decodes both sides with decodeAbiParameters and compares element-wise bigint equality. Precedent: test/signers/mldsa-encoding.pk-transform.kat.test.ts:146-204.

preparePublicKeyForDeployment signature is single-factory for falcon-eth (keccakXofFactory only, accepted for NFR-11 symmetry but unused internally because falcon's forward-NTT is deterministic over raw pk). Distinct from ml-dsa-eth's two-factory binding. pkToNttCompact delegates to encodePublicKeyForZKNOX then decodes the uint256[] wrapper to recover the bigint[] shape.

FalconEthAccount.sol: 127 LOC structural mirror of MlDsaEthAccount.sol; wrapper-path import `./imports/FalconRef.sol` (NOT direct ETHFALCON/src/); _VERIFY_SELECTOR = bytes4(keccak256('verify(bytes,bytes32,bytes)')) — ADL-ambiguity-safe; try/catch → SignatureMalformed() on verifier revert; SIG_VALIDATION_FAILED on crypto failure.
FalconRef.sol SHA (9043bb6...9c) byte-preserved across all T1-T6 (AC-11 + AC-14 regression guards). No submodule-immutability violation.

test/fixtures/falcon-eth.ts: 98 LOC mirror of test/fixtures/mldsa-eth.ts; two-step simulate+write for setKey; 20-byte SSTORE2 pointer-length check; cross-fixture network-sharing pattern (optional viem parameter).

T4 G6 happy path: 100 KAT vectors via signWithKatBytes + EntryPoint-impersonated simulateValidateUserOp + publicClient.estimateContractGas for AC-3 gas cap (16_777_216 HH3 tx_gas_limit_cap). Gas margin min=1,566,310 max=1,569,467 avg=1,567,713 — 10.7x headroom, <0.2% spread (ZKNOX verify is gas-constant). TDD smoke N=1 GREEN before N=100 expand.
T4 AC-FLOW-1: 5 fresh-keypair iterations via production keygen + signUserOp — proves production surfaces compose end-to-end, not just KAT paths.

T5 dual-path SignatureMalformed walker EXTRACTED to test/utils/signature-malformed-walker.ts (63 LOC) at 2-copy threshold per retrospect/typescript.md §'Duplicated test-file code'. assertSignatureMalformedForAccount(address) closure binds to address.toLowerCase() and dual-paths (canonical ContractFunctionRevertedError.errorName + HH3 EDR message-regex fallback). SignatureMalformed selector 0x2c3c2fe1 collides across 4 account contracts — address bind is the discriminator.

T5 TDD RED→GREEN on AC-6 address-bind proof: wrong 0xffff address binds to false; correct account.address binds to true. Load-bearing. Clever detour: initial 0x0000 attempt accidentally PASSED because 100-zero-byte malformed signature hex trivially contains zero-address as substring — switched to 0xff for clean RED signal.

T6 SCHEMES extended 4→5 ('falcon-eth' added); SCHEME_DEPLOYERS: Record<Scheme, Deployer> in test/signers/deployers.ts (175 LOC) extracts the deployAccount if-cascade. TS exhaustiveness check + runtime Object.keys().length === SCHEMES.length guard. Bench harness shrank ~100 LOC (if-cascade collapsed to registry lookup).

T6 gas pairing: falcon-eth verify is 1,536,489 gas — -68.9% vs mldsa-eth (4,946,763), ~3.2x cheaper than any other PQC path. Keccak-256 HashToPoint (A-006 Strategy E fork injection) pays off massively on-chain. Within-pair AC-7 bounds: mldsa-deltas 0.06% (vs 5% cap), falcon-deltas 2.19% (vs 25% cap). Falcon and falcon-eth share 1064 B calldata; mldsa and mldsa-eth share 2420 B.

T6 AC-13 deterministic bench: sha256sum docs/gas-report.md byte-stable across repeated `npm run report` invocations (no wall-clock leaks in renderer). Snapshot timestamp sourced from test/bench/gas-data.json, not Date.now().

T6 AC-10 naming discipline: test/signers/naming.test.ts walks src/test/contracts/scripts for lowercase `falcon_eth`; 0 hits. SCREAMING_SNAKE constants (FALCON_ETH_SIGNATURE_LENGTH, FALCON_ETH_FILE, FALCON_ETH_KAT_FILE) follow Story 2-3 convention — AC-10 targets lowercase only.

Shared test helpers consolidated under test/utils/: assert-bytes.ts (Story 2-2+) and signature-malformed-walker.ts (T5). Module-scope divergence-message helpers per retrospect/typescript.md §'Duplicated test-file code'.
deviations: Rule 3 (T1): A-007 amendment — AC-1 oracle shape changed from raw Hex byte-equality to structural coefficient-equality. Architecture anticipated the divergence-probe fallback at docs/architecture.md:199 ('pick structural-decode oracle up front'). T1 code preserved as-is; test rewritten to structural decode; AC text + must_have truth amended inline; sweep grep clean post-commit.

Rule 1 (T6): test/scripts/generate-report.test.ts fixture extended 4→5 entries — forced correctness edit after SCHEMES grew; renderReport count-validation requires 5 schemes for 5-scheme snapshot. Auto-fixed inline; not in T6's original file manifest but a necessary ripple.
openIssues: Pre-existing inaccuracy (out of 2-4 scope): docs/stories/1-1.md:342 describes fixture reshapedPublicKey as 'abi-encoded uint256[]' — actual layout is uint256[32] per scripts/generate-kat-fixtures.ts:1304 + A-007. Flagged in A-007; retrospective amendment of signed-off Story 1-1 is out of scope.

Pre-existing tsc --noEmit errors at test/signers/keccak-prg.ts:140,177 (ml-dsa-eth era, commit 9b7481a). Not part of hardhat test flow; no baseline failing-test-count impact. Not introduced by any 2-4 task; deferred to a future hardening pass.

tasks.name: Fork apply + repo wiring + falcon512paddedEth instantiation

patternsEstablished: Strategy E fork-injection: our repo instantiates `falcon512paddedEth = genFalcon({ ...falcon512paddedOpts, hashToPoint: hashToPointEVM })`; fork exports only `genFalcon` + `falcon512paddedOpts` (NOT base `falcon512opts`).
BytesReader interface in falcon-eth.kat-internal.ts abstracts signing randomness — KAT uses rngAesCtrDrbg256-driven reader, production uses crypto.getRandomValues-backed reader.
SIGNING_BYTES_EXHAUSTED guard pattern at randomness-source boundary — present in both `signWithKatBytes` (KAT) and `signUserOp` (production) callbacks; matches errors.ts JSDoc.

AC-7 boundary mirrors ml-dsa-eth: falcon-eth.ts (production) and falcon-eth.kat-internal.ts (KAT) do NOT import each other; both route through falcon-eth.core.js (falcon512paddedEth) + falcon-encoding.js (encodeSignatureForZKNOX).

Two-repo commit discipline: fork commits staged + committed locally, user pushes, our-repo commits land the pin. Branch pin (#falcon-eth-hashtopoint-injection) per user instruction; no merge to fork main; work-in-progress posture.
deviations: Rule 1 (T2): renamed falcon512Eth → falcon512paddedEth mid-commit to match noble's falcon512padded naming convention (our variant extends padded, not unpadded). Sweep across 6 files in the T2 commit.

Rule 2 (T2 Step A): fork diff raw shortstat is 33+/27- = 60 LOC vs story's ≤25 bound; semantic is ~7 new LOC (the 27/27 is re-indentation of preserved 25-line SHAKE256 body). Accepted; logged for Gate 5.
Rule 1 (T2 Step A): falcon512paddedOpts got explicit ': FalconOpts' annotation due to fork's `isolatedDeclarations: true` tsconfig setting.

Rule 2 (T3): falcon-eth.ts#signUserOp does NOT import from kat-internal (AC-7 boundary). Prompt had it routing through signWithKatBytes; corrected to mirror ml-dsa-eth.ts pattern — production calls falcon512paddedEth.sign + encodeSignatureForZKNOX directly.
Rule 2 (T3): signWithKatBytes guard also checks returned-chunk length (not just requested length) so AC-5 SIGNING_BYTES_EXHAUSTED has a testable path via a buggy reader.
Rule 1 (T3 revise): signUserOp guard added to close the JSDoc-vs-behavior gap on errors.ts documentation; defense-in-depth.
openIssues: T2 Step A: fork raw diff 60 LOC exceeds story Gate-5 sanity check #3 (≤25). Semantic diff ~7 LOC; relies on `git diff -w` for audit. Flag at Gate 5.
T3: signUserOp SIGNING_BYTES_EXHAUSTED guard is present but structurally untestable on the happy path (fixed 88 B buffer + noble's 88 B consumption). Covered defensively; no independent unit test.
Fork branch `falcon-eth-hashtopoint-injection` is pinned (not merged to main); work-in-progress posture per user instruction. ml-dsa-eth migration to the same fork deferred to a separate ops task.
